summary: Created a minimal dependency-ordered M3 task DAG covering the Roadmap M3 polity, office, character, vassalage, postwar governance, succession, UI/read-model, historical claim pipeline, and validation requirements without implementing M3 functionality.

tests_run.result: Pre-existing modified coordination files remain: project/goal-mode-state.json, project/model-routing-state.json, project/tasks/thread-registry.json. New untracked M3 task files are present under project/tasks/active, and this systems handoff is present under project/messages/outbox.
Listed prior M0/M1/M2 tasks as CLOSED; M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 as REVIEW; 12 downstream M3 tasks as DRAFT.
No output while M3-TASK-GRAPH-001 remains in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON validation for all new M3 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency existence/status shape
tests_run.result: M3 JSON DAG validation ok files=13 edges=36.

decisions: All downstream M3 implementation and validation tasks remain DRAFT; no M3 implementation task was advanced to READY.

The first ready candidates after graph acceptance are the historical claim pipeline and polity/vassalage substrate; character/office fixture work depends on both to avoid unsourced names/titles/relationships and missing polity references.

Simulation work is split into polity/vassalage, administrative burden, appointment/enfeoffment/policy/reason scaffolds, tribute/troop obligations, minimal succession, postwar governance, and explicit M3 save/runtime support.
Client appointment/bulk/reason UI depends on accepted sim/protocol reason and obligation work so the UI does not duplicate sim-core rules.
H-003/H-005 initial validation depends on the postwar prototype, client reason UI, and M3 save/runtime support; M3 exit validation depends on the full M3 graph.
No roadmap, accepted ADR, gate document, product code, package code, content-source data, or formal historical content was changed by this planning task.
risks: Existing coordination-state changes were present before this writer edited files and remain outside this task's authored changes.

Several downstream M3 tasks are R3 because they may touch protocol, save/runtime schema, deterministic sim-core state, UI read-model boundaries, or historical/cultural claim workflow; each task routes to systems_architect or qa_reviewer as appropriate.

M3-SAVE-RUNTIME-001 is included as a dependency because M3 introduces explicit authoritative state slices; it is scoped to DTO/schema persistence and explicitly excludes platform persistence or Electron/Node IO.
H-003/H-005 validation is initial M3 evidence only and must not be overstated as M5 player-study proof.
requested_action: Review the M3 DAG for Roadmap/acceptance-matrix completeness, dependency ordering, architecture-boundary discipline, historical claim-pipeline safety, downstream DRAFT status, and handoff validity. Accept or request changes.
